title: Dromund system
content:
{{Top|canon=Dromund Kaas system}} {{System |image=[[File:Dromund_system.png]] |name=Dromund system |region=*[[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *[[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Trans-Hydian]]The [[:File:MiscellaneousRegions.jpg|Client States and Miscellaneous Regions map]] in ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' places the entirety of grid square R-5 in the [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Trans-Hydian]]. |sector=[[Esstran sector/Legends|Esstran sector]]''[[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion]]''{{'s}} entry for the [[Drezzi system/Legends|Drezzi system]] places sith worlds in the Esstran sector *[[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith Worlds]] |coordinates=R-5 |xyz= |quadrants= |suns=[[Dromund]]{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050609a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 5: Two Peas in a Pod}} |orbits=#[[Dromund Ixin]] #[[Dromund Kalakar]] #*6 moons #[[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas]] #*2 moons #[[Dromund Fels]] #*1 moon #[[Dromund Tyne]] |stations=[[Emperor's Fortress]]{{TORcite|type=class|Jedi Knight|Guided by the Force|Tatooine}} |asteroids= |comets= |nebulae= |routes=[[Kamat Krote]] |other= |species= |otherspecies=*[[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]]''[[The Old Republic: Revan]]'' *[[Human/Legends|Humans]] |socialgroup= |language=[[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] |population=10,000 {{C|Modern}} |imports=None {{C|Modern}} |exports=None {{C|Modern}} |affiliation=[[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Empire]]{{Holonet|planets|dromund-kaas|Dromund Kaas}} }} {{Quote|Your family's from Dromund Kaas, aren't they, sir?''"
"''The Dromund system is just a myth.''"
"''If you say so, sir.|[[Clone commando/Legends|Imperial commando]] [[Darman Skirata|Darman]] and [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Commander/Legends|commander]] [[Roly Melusar]]|''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]''}} The '''Dromund system''', also known as the '''Dromund Kaas system''', was a [[Star system/Legends|star system]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories's]] [[Esstran sector/Legends|Esstran sector]] that lay within the region of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] known as the [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith Worlds]]. A five-[[Planet/Legends|planet]] system that contained a single [[Star/Legends|star]], [[Dromund]], the Dromund system was historically associated with various [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] organizations. While two of the system's planets, [[Dromund Ixin]] and [[Dromund Tyne]], were barren and a third, [[Dromund Kalakar]], was a [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]], the two other worlds—the arid [[Dromund Fels]] and the terrestrial [[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas]]—could support life, and Dromund Kaas served as the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Sith Empire]] for over a thousand [[Standard year/Legends|years]]. However, the Dromund system was largely unknown to [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] at large throughout most of history. ==Description== The Dromund system was a [[Star system/Legends|star system]] located in the [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith Worlds]], a region of the [[Esstran sector/Legends|Esstran sector]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} The system contained one [[Star/Legends|star]], a yellow sun named [[Dromund]], and five [[Planet/Legends|planets]]: [[Dromund Ixin]], [[Dromund Kalakar]], [[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas]], [[Dromund Fels]], and [[Dromund Tyne]]. Located within the [[Stygian Caldera]] nebula, the Dromund system was connected to [[Bosthirda/Legends|Bosthirda]] and [[Jaguada/Legends|Jaguada]] by the [[Kamat Krote]] hyperlane. The [[Tenebrae|Sith Emperor]] of the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Sith Empire]] maintained a [[Emperor's Fortress|personal space station]] in orbit over Dromund Kaas for much of his reign, though the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Malgus]] stole the [[Space station/Legends|space station]] after the Emperor's supposed [[Death/Legends|death]] and moved it to the [[Ilum system/Legends|Ilum system]].{{TORcite|type=flashpoint|The False Emperor}} The Stygian Caldera nebula was visible from within the star system, painting the fabric of space a reddish-brown [[Color/Legends|color]].{{TORcite|type=class|Jedi Knight|Descent|the Emperor's Fortress}} ==History== [[File:Kamat_Krote.png|thumb|left|160px|Dromund Kaas and the Kamat Krote hyperlane]] {{Quote|There are some interesting historical aspects to this world. Things that perhaps only you and I know, depending on what you've learned from Ship. Such as the fact that this world was a colony of the Sith Empire, but faded into obscurity until after the Great Hyperspace War. It was rediscovered by a Sith armada wandering for two decades.|Luke Skywalker, on the history of Dromund Kaas|''[[Fate of the Jedi: Ascension]]''}} The Dromund system was discovered by the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]], the civilization of the [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]] [[Species/Legends|species]] from the planet [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], sometime before the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[6900 BBY]].''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]'' [[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas]] was colonized by the Empire early in its history. However, the colony and the entire system were eventually lost to history along with the Kamat Kroke route by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]] with the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] in [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]]. The system was rediscovered by researchers on the planet [[Nathema]] under the orders of the Sith Lord Vitiate in [[4999 BBY]],''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]'' and he led the remains of the shattered Sith civilization on a [[Sith exodus|exodus]] from Korriban that lasted nearly twenty years before ending in the Dromund system. On Dromund Kaas, Vitiate—now the [[Immortality/Legends|immortal]] Sith Emperor—reconstituted the Empire, and the star system became the epicenter of the new civilization. In [[3642 BBY]],{{TOR_dates|2}} the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Jomar Chul]] scouted the Dromund system in order to determine the defense of the Emperor's space station, and though he was forced to flee the system to escape Imperial [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]], his data was used by a [[Jedi strike team (capture of the Sith Emperor)|Jedi strike team]] to infiltrate the system and confront the Emperor aboard his space station. In [[3640 BBY]], during the [[Galactic War]] between the Republic and the Empire, the Dromund system was the site of a [[Dromund Kaas operation|battle]] between the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] and the Empire's [[First Defense Fleet]]. The Republic Navy's attack was a distraction so that the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] known as the [[Hero of Tython]] could infiltrate Dromund Kaas and eliminate the Emperor, and the Republic [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] withdrew to the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] after the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] succeeded in the operation.{{TORcite|type=class|Jedi Knight|Doomsday|Dromund Kaas}} [[File:Dromund_tor.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A star chart of the system]] By the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[2000 BBY/Legends|2000 BBY]], the Dromund system was once more abandoned, and though Dromund Kaas was the site of a [[Battle of Dromund Kaas (New Sith Wars)|battle]] in [[1004 BBY]] between the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and the Sith [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]], the system remained uninhabited until sometime after [[990 BBY]], when the rogue Sith Lord [[Darth Millennial]] fled to Dromund Kaas and established the [[Dark Force]] religion there. The Dark Force's followers, the [[Prophets of the Dark Side]],{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/88/indexp2.html|text=''Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties''|int=Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties}} ruled the few inhabitants of Dromund Kaas and remained unknown to the galaxy at large until [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], when the Sith Lord [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] discovered the Prophets on Dromund Kaas. Shortly before that time, a [[Human/Legends|Human]] attempted to start a revolt against the Prophets, though he was killed. As a result, his son [[Roly Melusar]] developed a strong hatred of [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitives]], and he told anyone who asked him that the Dromund system was a myth. The system and its planets were believed to be mythical by [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] at large, but the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] confirmed the system's existence during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] between the Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], and the Republic's [[327th Star Corps/Legends|327th Star Corps]] fought a [[Battle of Dromund Kaas|battle]] on Dromund Kaas during the conflict.{{InsiderCite|84|Guide to the Grand Army of the Republic}} In the [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] that followed, the system was visited at least twice by members of the [[New Jedi Order]]—first in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], when the [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] [[Mission to Dromund Kaas (New Republic)|rescued]] her teacher [[Kyle Katarn]] from Dromund Kaas after the Jedi Knight was driven to the dark side by the planet;''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Mysteries of the Sith]]'' and in [[44 ABY]], when [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], his son [[Ben Skywalker]], [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]], and [[Vestara Khai]] were [[Duel on Dromund Kaas|ambushed]] by a group of [[Sith Saber]]s under the leadership of Vestara's father [[Gavar Khai]] on Dromund Kaas. ==Orbital bodies== ===Dromund Ixin=== {{Main|Dromund Ixin}} '''Dromund Ixin''' was a barren world without satellites, the closest to the star Dromund. ===Dromund Kalakar=== {{Main|Dromund Kalakar}} '''Dromund Kalakar''' was the second planet in the Dromund system, a [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] orbited by six [[Moon/Legends|moons]]. One of these moons, the volcanic [[Kalakar Six]], was a mining colony of the reconstituted Empire, though an eruption buried the settlement in ash.{{TORcite|type=crewskill|Archaeology|A Silent City}} Thousands of years later, the Prophets of the Dark Side lured the Sith Lord [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] to the moon and its powerful [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] [[Vergence/Legends|nexus]], where they [[Duel on Kalakar Six|pitted]] a doppelgänger of the Sith Lord [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]] against Vader.{{Tales|9|Resurrection (comic story)}} ===Dromund Kaas=== [[File:DromundKaas-TOR.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Dromund Kaas]] {{Main|Dromund Kaas/Legends}} {{Quote|Welcome to Dromund Kaas, where freedom goes to die, and legends are forced on the galaxy.|[[Vette]]|{{TORcite|type=location|Ambient diaologue from Vette|Dromund Kaas}}}} The third planet in the Dromund system, Dromund Kaas had two moons and was a world of jungles and swamps that hosted significant Sith presences throughout much of galactic history. As a result of [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]] performed by the Emperor of the reconstituted Sith Empire, the planet's [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] was warped so that Dromund Kaas was constantly shrouded in storms, and it was the site of a powerful dark side nexus. Originally a colony of the first Sith Empire, Dromund Kaas was rediscovered by the reconstituted Empire and served as its capital for over a thousand years, though it was largely abandoned and only featured scattered populations of Sith and dark side users. ===Dromund Fels=== {{Main|Dromund Fels}} {{Quote|Igrol resides on Dromund Fels.|[[Darth Nyriss]], one of Igrol's fellow Dark Councilors|}} Dromund Fels was an arid planet that occupied the fourth orbit around Dromund, and it was orbited by one moon. [[Darth Igrol]], a member of the reconstituted Empire's [[Dark Council]], maintained a residence on Dromund Fels during his lifetime, and the archaeologists of the [[Imperial Reclamation Service]] conducted numerous excavations on the world throughout the Empire's history. ===Dromund Tyne=== {{Main|Dromund Tyne}} The fifth planet in the Dromund system, '''Dromund Tyne''' was a barren planet like Dromund Ixin that possessed no moons. ==Appearances== *''[[The Old Republic: Revan]]'' {{Ab|The Old Republic: Revan (audiobook)}} *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Rise of the Hutt Cartel]]'' *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Galactic Strongholds]]'' *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Knights of the Fallen Empire]]'' *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Knights of the Eternal Throne]]'' *''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]'' *{{Tales|9|Resurrection (comic story)}} ==Sources== *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050609a|series=The Dark Forces
